label: Token Kitchen
subject: Serving LLM requests fast — why vLLM exists
bossHook: Turn one slow model into an engine that serves hundreds of users at once — in an order that actually works.

order-matters.title: First, Load the Model
order-matters.hook: You can't answer a prompt you haven't received, on a model you haven't loaded. Put these three in a working order.
order-matters.winText: Load → receive → generate. The model must be on the GPU before it can serve, and a request must arrive before it can answer. Order matters.

prerequisite.title: The Foundation
prerequisite.hook: Some steps unlock others. Find the one thing that must happen first, then build on it.
prerequisite.winText: The loaded model came first — the KV cache and the request queue both needed it, and batching needed the cache. That's a prerequisite.

failText.wrong-start: You started with a step that needs something else first. Begin with loading the model — it needs nothing.
failText.dependency-violation: That step ran before its prerequisite existed. A batch needs KV-cache space, and the cache needs the model.

the-chain.title: The Efficiency Chain
the-chain.hook: Batching and the scheduler both stand on the paged KV cache. Trace the chain that ends in high throughput.
the-chain.winText: Paging the KV cache freed the memory; batching and the scheduler both used it; together they deliver high throughput. That's the whole reason vLLM is fast.

failText.wrong-start: Start with the step that needs nothing — load the model.
failText.dependency-violation: You batched or scheduled before the paged KV cache existed. Memory comes first.

boss-pipeline.title: BOSS · Serve Hundreds at Once
boss-pipeline.hook: Six steps, two branches — memory and requests — that must converge. Build the whole serving engine from a cold GPU.
boss-pipeline.winText: Two branches converged: paged memory made room, batching filled the GPU, and the scheduler kept it full. One model now serves hundreds at once — that's why vLLM is used.

failText.wrong-start: Begin with the step that needs nothing — load the model.
failText.dependency-violation: A step ran before something it depends on. Trace what each needs — memory before scheduling, requests before batching.

missing-foundation.title: SIDE-QUEST · Load First
missing-foundation.hook: You keep building before the base exists. Drill it: place the ONE thing everything else needs, first.
missing-foundation.winText: Model first, then the rest. The foundation is whatever needs nothing and unlocks everything — here, the loaded model.

failText.wrong-start: Still starting in the wrong place — lead with the model; it has no prerequisites.
failText.dependency-violation: Put the loaded model before the cache and the queue that depend on it.
